title: vendor latest containers/{buildah,image}
body:
Due to code dependencies, both need to be vendored simultaneously.
The new changes mostly relate to caching and are a first step towards
faster pulls.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

```
# github.com/containers/libpod/test/bin2img
test/bin2img/bin2img.go:159:28: not enough arguments in call to img.PutBlob
	have (context.Context, *bytes.Buffer, types.BlobInfo, bool)
	want (context.Context, io.Reader, types.BlobInfo, types.BlobInfoCache, bool)
test/bin2img/bin2img.go:187:32: not enough arguments in call to img.PutBlob
	have (context.Context, *bytes.Buffer, types.BlobInfo, bool)
	want (context.Context, io.Reader, types.BlobInfo, types.BlobInfoCache, bool)
```

Do we still need this tool? It looks an ancient relict but I am not 100% sure. @rhatdan ?
I updated bin2img now. The rebase issue was odd as I force pushed without changing the base. Not sure if it's in my setup or if GitHub is going wild. It happened twice today but never before.

@vrothberg ubuntu tests aren't happy.  I'm a bit confused between the description and the code changes.  You say "both need to be vendored" and yet I only see one file being removed which in itself I'm a little suspicious of?

> @vrothberg ubuntu tests aren't happy. I'm a bit confused between the description and the code changes. You say "both need to be vendored" and yet I only see one file being removed which in itself I'm a little suspicious of?

I grabbed a tea and try to figure out how I messed up my local repo. I must have missed changing the branch while working on another PR. Thanks for the hint, @TomSweeneyRedHat!

Seems to be missing changes to vendor.conf?
